page.content: The data presented in the table provides a comprehensive analysis of the performance metrics associated with the CHSA and GP variables. Specifically, the reported cGPAB values, along with their 95% confidence intervals (CIs) and corresponding p-values, serve as critical indicators of the effectiveness of the interventions being assessed. For instance, the cGPAB for CHSA shows a mean of 0.21 with a confidence interval ranging from -0.14 to 0.56, accompanied by a p-value of 0.17.

lines.text: The data presented in the table provides a comprehensive analysis of the performance metrics associated with the CHSA and
GP variables. Specifically, the reported cGPAB values, along with their 95% confidence intervals (CIs) and corresponding p-
values, serve as critical indicators of the effectiveness of the interventions being assessed. For instance, the cGPAB for CHSA
shows a mean of 0.21 with a confidence interval ranging from -0.14 to 0.56, accompanied by a p-value of 0.17.

page.content: This suggests that while there is a positive average effect, the result is not statistically significant at the conventional threshold. Conversely, the Progress testB metric for CHSA reflects a mean of 0.31, with a confidence interval of -12.03 to 12.66 and a p-value of 0.95, indicating a lack of significant impact in this area as well. The variability in these results, as evidenced by the standard deviation and range of observations, underscores the complexity of the data and highlights the necessity for further investigation. As such, this analysis serves as a foundational component for understanding the intricate relationships between the evaluated variables and their broader implications within the context of the study.
